steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option in the "Add an action to your declarative agent" dropdown within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog in Microsoft Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "None" option in the "Select an authentication type" dropdown menu within the "Authentication Type" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "Default folder: /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" field within the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or modify the project directory path.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# File path
FILE_PATH="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_hdNtv/appPackage/ai-plugin.json"

# Verify that the file exists
if [[ -f "${FILE_PATH}" ]]; then
    # Update the specified line of the file.
    sed -i '4s/name_for_human/name_for_humanaaaa/' "${FILE_PATH}"
    echo "Renamed 'name_for_human' to 'name_for_humanaaaa' on line 4."
else
    echo "File not found: ${FILE_PATH}"
    exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Renamed 'name_for_human' to 'name_for_humanaaaa' on line 4.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description:
@code in file: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/ai-plugin.json"
rename in the line 4  'name_for_human'  to  'name_for_humanaaaa'
@assertion 'Error: property name must be valid. Details: {"propertyName":"name_for_humanaaa}' is exist
